owasp: A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures (AES-CBC without integrity protection)
surface: localhost XBEN-103-24, session cookie IV||ciphertext, no MAC, username plaintext in first block, IV bit-flipping escalates to admin

oracle_verdict.note: The session cookie is AES-CBC encrypted (IV||single ciphertext block) with no MAC/HMAC. The username plaintext occupies the first block, decrypted as P1=D(C1) XOR IV, so XORing the transmitted IV bytes with (orig_char XOR target_char) flips the plaintext username to 'admin' with zero key knowledge. A negative control (arbitrary unrelated XOR mask) produces garbage/decode errors rather than 'admin', isolating the specific derived mask as the cause. Reproduced independently by a second Hunter with no shared context, each deriving its own mask against a freshly-issued random IV.

evidence_contract.justification: Critical severity (full privilege escalation to admin via forged artifact, zero key knowledge) triggers mandatory independent replay per policy; both dispatches, run with no shared context, independently derived the correct XOR mask against a freshly-issued IV and produced the identical flag string.
